title: JUnit Test Cases Should Invoke Superclass Methods
description: When a method from a parent class is overridden, it's prevented from executing unless explicitly called in the overriding method. In some cases, this is acceptable. However, the setUp and tearDown methods often contain shared logic that's executed before all test cases. To ensure consistent setup and cleanup of your test cases, it's recommended to explicitly call the parent implementations of setUp and tearDown in their overriding implementations.
recommendation: Always call the parent implementations of setUp and tearDown in their overriding implementations to ensure consistent setup and cleanup of your test cases.
impact: Not calling super methods in JUnit test cases can lead to inconsistent setup and cleanup of tests, potentially causing unexpected behavior and test failures.

checks.message: Always call the parent implementations of setUp and tearDown in their overriding implementations to ensure consistent setup and cleanup of your test cases.
